title: Function and Method Hooking Flow

api_signatures.hook_method: DDTrace\hook_method(string $className, string $methodName, Closure|array|null $prehookOrConfigArray = null, ?Closure $posthook = null): bool

closure_signatures.trace_method_closure: (SpanData $span, array $args, mixed $retval, Throwable|null $exception): void — bound to target object/scope

interaction_flow_trace_function: Integration::init() → DDTrace\trace_function($functionName, $tracingClosure) — registers hook with extension
DDTrace C extension → returns true (hook registered)
DDTrace C extension → intercepts call → opens SpanData (auto)
tracingClosure → SpanData: sets name, resource, service, meta, metrics tags
DDTrace C extension → closes SpanData (auto), records any exception
interaction_flow_trace_method: DDTrace C extension → intercepts → opens SpanData (auto)
DDTrace C extension → executes original method (closure bound to target object/scope)
interaction_flow_hook_method: DDTrace C extension → executes original method
interaction_flow_notify_start_commit: Integration::init() → DDTrace\hook_method/trace_method on framework request-handling method
notify_start → AppSec/WAF listeners: evaluate request, optionally block
notify_start → returns ?array{status, headers, body} (block response) or null (continue)
notify_commit → AppSec/WAF listeners: evaluate response, optionally replace
notify_commit → returns ?array{status, headers, body} (replacement) or null (pass-through)
integration_lifecycle: Integration::shouldLoad($name) → checks ddtrace extension loaded + ddtrace_config_integration_enabled($name)
Integration::init(): static — calls shouldLoad(); if false returns NOT_AVAILABLE
Integration::init() → returns LOADED (success) or NOT_LOADED (deferred) or NOT_AVAILABLE

key_methods: static shouldLoad(string $name): bool — checks extension + config
static handleOrphan(SpanData $span): void — sets AUTO_REJECT if orphan root span

key_relationships: Concrete integrations extend Integration and call hook_function/hook_method/trace_function/trace_method in init()
hook_function/hook_method provide raw pre/posthook access without span management
notify_start/notify_commit used by long-running-server integrations (Roadrunner, Ratchet, Frankenphp) for per-request AppSec lifecycle
SpanData is the core data object enriched by tracing closures (meta, metrics, name, resource, service)

key_differences.trace_vs_hook: trace_* auto-opens and closes a SpanData around the call; hook_* only runs closures with no automatic span
key_differences.prehook_vs_posthook: prehook fires before execution (no retval/exception available); posthook fires after (retval/exception available)
key_differences.notify_start_vs_notify_commit: notify_start fires at request ingress with request data; notify_commit fires at response egress with HTTP status+headers+body

real_integration_examples: PHPRedisIntegration: trace_method('Redis', 'connect', closure) — spans Redis connection calls
SlimIntegration: hook_method then trace_method('ClassName', 'process', closure) — dynamic middleware tracing
StripeIntegration: hook_method('Stripe\Service\...', 'create', null, $posthook) — posthook-only, no prehook
FrankenphpIntegration: hook_method on frankenphp_handle_request → notify_start + notify_commit for AppSec
RoadrunnerIntegration: hook_method on PSR-7 handle → notify_start on request, notify_commit on response emit
important_notes: All hooking functions are in the DDTrace namespace and implemented in the ddtrace C extension (ddtrace.stub.php is the PHP stub). notify_start/notify_commit are in DDTrace\UserRequest sub-namespace and bridge integration hooks to AppSec/WAF event listeners. Tracing closures in trace_* are bound to the target object (or scope for static methods). The config array form supports prehook + posthook together, with optional instrument_when_limited and recurse flags.
